entities.name: UPLAND RICE
entities.description: A type of rice grown in non-flooded upland areas

entities.name: GRAIN LEGUMES
entities.description: A group of nitrogen-fixing crops including cowpea, groundnut, and pigeon pea

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A specific grain legume used in intercropping systems

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A specific grain legume used in intercropping systems

entities.name: PIGEON PEA
entities.description: A specific grain legume used in intercropping systems

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously on the same piece of land

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: A type of clay-rich soil used as the growth medium in the study

entities.name: RICE YIELD
entities.description: The productivity measure of upland rice

entities.name: LEGUME YIELD
entities.description: The productivity measure of the intercropped legumes

entities.name: INTERCROPPING ADVANTAGE
entities.description: The measure of increased productivity in intercropping compared to monoculture

entities.name: HY 3C
entities.description: An indeterminate variety of pigeon pea

entities.name: IRRADIANCE
entities.description: The amount of solar radiation reaching the crop canopy

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The seasonal period of precipitation affecting crop growth

entities.name: GROWTH
entities.description: The developmental progress and biomass increase of the crops

entities.name: CANOPY COVER
entities.description: The extent to which the crop foliage covers the ground

entities.name: ERECT LEAF ARRANGEMENT
entities.description: The vertical orientation of leaves characteristic of upland rice

entities.name: RADIATION INTERCEPTED
entities.description: The amount of light energy captured by the crop canopy

entities.name: SPATIAL COMPLEMENTARITY
entities.description: The efficient use of space by different crops to maximize resource capture

entities.name: DETERMINATE LEGUMES
entities.description: Legumes with a limited growth habit, identified as more suitable for intercropping
